title: Data Fellowship
source: Authored for the Salareen corporate catalog; structured to mirror the UK apprenticeship standard format.

knowledge.K1: The data analyst workflow: question, gather, clean, analyse and communicate.
knowledge.K2: How to turn vague business requests into specific, decision-linked questions.
knowledge.K3: SQL for filtering, aggregating and joining data from relational databases.
knowledge.K4: Data modelling concepts: tables, keys, relationships and tidy data.
knowledge.K5: Data cleaning: handling missing values, duplicates and inconsistencies.
knowledge.K6: Programming for analysis (e.g. Python and pandas) for repeatable work.
knowledge.K7: Descriptive statistics: mean, median, spread and distributions.
knowledge.K8: Exploratory data analysis to build intuition and form hypotheses.
knowledge.K9: Correlation versus causation and the role of statistical significance.
knowledge.K11: How to communicate insight and a recommendation to decision-makers.
knowledge.K12: Responsible, ethical and reproducible handling of data.

skills.S1: Frame a sharp, answerable analytical question.
skills.S2: Write SQL queries to assemble the required dataset.
skills.S3: Model and reshape data into a clean, analysable structure.
skills.S4: Clean data and document the choices made.
skills.S5: Use code to transform, summarise and chart data repeatably.
skills.S6: Apply descriptive statistics and interpret distributions honestly.
skills.S7: Conduct exploratory data analysis to surface patterns and issues.
skills.S8: Avoid over-claiming by distinguishing correlation from causation.
skills.S9: Create honest visualisations and focused dashboards.
skills.S10: Communicate findings with a clear headline and recommendation.
skills.S11: Protect personal data and follow data-protection policy.
skills.S12: Make analysis reproducible and assumptions explicit.

behaviours.B1: Curious and rigorous in investigating data.
behaviours.B2: Takes responsibility for the accuracy of analysis.
behaviours.B3: Communicates and collaborates clearly with stakeholders.
behaviours.B4: Acts ethically and with integrity around data and bias.
behaviours.B5: Comfortable working with uncertainty and incomplete data.

Duty 1.statement: Frame analytical questions from business needs.

Duty 2.statement: Acquire and prepare data for analysis.

Duty 3.statement: Analyse data with code and statistics.

Duty 4.statement: Explore data and avoid analytical traps.

Duty 5.statement: Visualise and communicate insight and recommendations.

Duty 6.statement: Work responsibly and reproducibly with data.
